channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 3 ways Tesla stands to win from Elon Musk’s war on the U.S. government
item.description: Elon Musk has long railed against the U.S. government, saying a crushing number of federal investigations and safety programs have stymied Tesla, his electric car company, and its efforts to create fleets of robotaxis and other self-driving automobiles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Apple’s security patch highlights the growing security threat
item.description: Apple’s platforms may be more secure by design than others, but that doesn’t make them invulnerable to attack. That’s why every user should install the company’s latest security patch — it fixes a hole Apple says may already have been in active use.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Russian cybercrime network targeted for sanctions across US, UK and Australia
item.description: The U.S., U.K. and Australia have sanctioned a Russian web-hosting services provider and two Russian men who administer the service in support of Russian ransomware syndicate LockBit.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance rips 'excessive' AI regulation at Paris summit
item.description: Vice President Vance slammed "excessive regulation" of artificial intelligence (AI) on Tuesday, making clear the Trump administration's stance on the emerging tech on an international stage.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Did you turn off Apple Intelligence? Updating to iOS 18.3.1 or macOS 15.3.1 might’ve turned it on again
item.description: iOS 18.3.1 and macOS 15.3.1 bug causing Apple Intelligence to get reactivated despite users switching it off in the past.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: Musk's $97.4 Billion Offer to Acquire OpenAI Rejected
item.description: Elon Musk's bid to acquire nonprofit controlling OpenAI rejected, sparking concerns about the future of ChatGPT maker and Microsoft's cloud partnership.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rivian opens sales of plug-in vans once exclusive to Amazon
item.description: If it gains traction, the commercial van could become a bigger source of revenue amid a broader slowdown in consumer EV uptake.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Debate about political CIOs takes on new life with OPM memo
item.description: Agencies have until Friday to decide if they want to reclassify their CIOs as “general” Senior Executive Service positions instead of “career reserve.” The post Debate about political CIOs takes on new life with OPM memo first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk furious as judge shuts down DOGE access to Treasury payment system
item.description: The US Treasury Department’s payment servers hold the tax returns, social security data and bank account numbers of every adult citizen of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming a prime target for sophisticated scammers
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are using Microsoft Teams for phishing, vishing and quishing via social engineering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Windows PowerToys: Your handy productivity toolbox
item.description: Back in the 1990s, Windows power users often availed themselves of a set of small, free software tools from Microsoft collectively known as “PowerToys.” (Read about their history at Wikipedia.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
